response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective and Green Map GIS projects, and has demonstrated ability to work on high-throughput distributed systems at Dunnhumby. However, the role's core requirement is modern C++ (or Rust) for routing data pipeline engineering, and his C++ experience is both minimal and dated — limited to a 2014–2015 stint at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project. His current and recent stack is almost entirely TypeScript/Node.js/JavaScript with no Rust whatsoever, and he has no experience with routing tiles, navigation pipelines, or traffic data integration, which are central to the role.
strengths: Geospatial domain experience: built and maintains GISCollective (event-driven geo-data platform) and a GIS web app for Green Map, showing familiarity with the problem space
High-throughput, low-latency systems: worked on a micro-services infrastructure handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby, including async I/O and high-performance caching
Broad distributed-systems exposure: Kubernetes deployments, CI/CD pipelines, service-oriented architectures across multiple roles
concerns: C++ proficiency is minimal and stale: last substantive C++ work was at National Instruments in 2014–2015; no evidence of modern C++ (C++17/20) in commercial settings
No Rust experience at all, eliminating the only alternative language path the role accepts
Zero experience with routing pipelines, navigation guidance systems, or geospatial tile generation — the core technical domain of the role
